summary: Formatting-only cleanup PR that ran `make format`. The only available body detail says it mostly added newlines to empty files. No changed file list, diff excerpt, reviews, or comments were present in the slice, so there is no evidence of functional decompilation changes.
what_changed: Ran the repository formatter via `make format`.
According to the PR body excerpt, the formatting pass mostly added newlines to empty files.
No changed files or diff excerpt were included in the provided PR slice.
smart_moves: Kept formatting churn isolated in a dedicated PR, making it easier to separate mechanical style changes from functional decompilation work.
decomp_lessons: Run `make format` separately or before opening functional PRs to avoid mixing whitespace-only changes with matching or code-structure changes.
Empty source or header files should still satisfy formatter expectations, including trailing-newline conventions where required.
follow_up_queries: Find PRs that introduced empty files before PR 1221 and may have triggered the formatter newline cleanup.
